title: Letter Grades — Conditional Logic
description: Add an if/else chain that converts the numeric average into a letter grade: A (90+), B (80+), C (70+), D (60+), F (below 60). Display the letter grade alongside the numeric average.
test_code:
assert 'if' in cpp, 'Must use if statement for grade conditions'
assert 'else' in cpp, 'Must use else for alternative conditions'
cpp_after_main = cpp.split('main')[1] if 'main' in cpp else cpp
assert '>=' in cpp_after_main or '>' in cpp_after_main, 'Must use comparison operators (>= or >) for grade thresholds'
assert 'letterGrade' in cpp or 'letter_grade' in cpp or 'letter' in cpp, 'Must store the letter grade in a variable'
assert cpp_after_main.count('if') >= 2, 'Need at least 2 if/else if conditions for the grade scale'
acceptance_criteria: Uses if/else chain to convert average to letter grade
Covers all grade ranges: A (90+), B (80+), C (70+), D (60+), F
Displays both the numeric average and letter grade
hints: Start with the highest grade: if (average >= 90) letterGrade = 'A';
Chain with else if: else if (average >= 80) letterGrade = 'B';
End with else for F: else letterGrade = 'F';
Use char type for a single letter: char letterGrade;
